concept.headline: Loops are valid but must be gas-aware.
concept.explanation: Each iteration costs gas, so avoid unbounded loops on growing storage collections.
concept.syntax: for (uint256 i = 1; i <= n; i++) { s += i; }
keyPoints: Large loops can hit block gas limits
Prefer off-chain iteration for big datasets

demo.intro: Compute bounded arithmetic sum with for-loop.

explanation.misconception: returns named variable initializes automatically to 0
Too-large n can make tx fail due to gas

prediction.question: Main risk of looping over very large on-chain arrays?
prediction.explanation: Large loops may exceed gas limits and revert the transaction.

explanation.afterDemo: Loop logic must always be designed with gas limits in mind for production contracts.

practice.prompt: Write pure function sum3() returning sum of 1..3 using a for loop.

summary.title: Loops — Complete
takeaways: Prefer off-chain iteration for large data
